entry.title: Re: Automatic storage location in outbound delivery for S...
entry.content:
Hello @cristianquinta 

Please note that the storage location is not determined within the sales document. As part of the standard process, it will always be automatically determined in the delivery document.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Question about Third Party Logistics Outbound Process...
entry.content: First of all, thank you for your response. That is very sad news. By the way, when creating an Outbound Delivery manually, you need to manually input both the External Delivery ID and External Delivery Item ID, right? This is very troublesome and customers get angry about it. Please tell me the solution, or how to input these automatically.

entry.title: Require Multiple times Log In (SAP B1)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
I need your help to resolve an Issue I'm facing while logging in into SAP B1 Client(GUI).
When logging into SAP Business One, I can access the system and see the modules under the toolbar. However, the home page, which includes dashboards and the left-side menu, prompts me to log in again. This additional login requirement disrupts the user experience.

Has anyone encountered this issue before? Any guidance on resolving it would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Can Host to Host bank integration be done using F-53?
entry.content:
Hello @Ramabhadran , if requirement is to just fill profit center try below options.

1. If document splitting is active, check PC is maintained in splitting character then ideally in payment document PC should be fetched from vendor invoice.

2. If 1 is not there/not possible then try some substitution rule to Fill in F110 documents.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow
entry.content: Thank you Sandra for sharing this post. I executed the report RSWDACTIVATE in SE38 with my workflow and found that Workflow is active and there are no errors shown. I have checked the binding and it is correct. Is there a way I can debug this issue.?

entry.title: Second Manager Import in Job relationship - Error: User cannot be their own manager
entry.content:
Hi All

In our current process we maintain Second Manager (Comp Planner) in Job Relationship. In some cases the User himself is their own comp planner.

Until last year we were able to use this solution and import the data successfully in Job Relationship. But after the 1H2024 release SAP has added a validation that User cannot be their own manager. (https://me.sap.com/notes/3460009/E)

We're ready to launch the forms, but we've encountered an error. Has anyone seen this before? If so, what's the best workaround?

Thank you

Viswa

entry.title: Re: [URGENT] All My Inbox apps in SAP BTP Build Work Zone...
entry.content: Thanks. recreating the SBPA instance service key worked.

entry.title: Re: Fetch duplicate records from database table
entry.content: Hello sandra, getting syntax error as "Field "COUNT(*)" unknown"

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana - House Bank Creation and Check Lot Creation

entry.title: Re: Deploying Langfuse on SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime
entry.content: Thank you for the blog. Its a great blog and very informative.

entry.title: Re: not added to the banking hierarchy
entry.content: I found out I can ignore this message... Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Is reverse Workflow for Budget Allocation Approval an...
entry.content: Thanks Xavier, appreciate it!

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Unmanaged scenario example-Simplified
entry.content:
Hi Anjan,

Thanks for giving this short and crisp overview of the code, This is very helpful for beginner's , provides how to implement various overwhelming methods for new commers.

Regards,

Basant

entry.title: Re: cHarm setup & ITSM setup
entry.content: Any update pls?

entry.title: Re: 2502 C4C Release Discussion
entry.content:
Hi All,

This has been released. Please use the link to access   https://static.hybris.com/audience_engagement/customer_newsletter/c4c/2025/2502_Roll_Out_SAP_Cloud_for_Customer_Consolidated.pdf the updates. Though there are no major updates.

Thanks,

Jayant Saini

entry.title: A TAX PREREQUISITE AMOUNT PAYMENT IS CUMULATIVE BUT IN THE EXEMPTION IT IS SHOWING FOR ONLY ONE MTH
entry.content:
WE ARE PAYING A ALLOWANCE   FOR TAX DEDUCED IN PREREQUISITE VALUES  WHICH ARE EXEMPTED UNDER SECTION 10.

BUT SYSTEM SHOWING ONLY EXEMPTION FOR THE CURRENT MONTH.

 

Please advice how to fix this problem

entry.title: Re: Ariba Contracts Integration with Fieldglass via Open APIs - restriction
entry.content: Hello Akshay, we have worked on contract compliance from third party contract management system. For that, there is a different procedure. But accessing data through APIs is not a problem at all.

entry.title: Re: Question about Third Party Logistics Outbound Process...
entry.content: Dear Customer, when creating 3PL outbound delivery manually, you need to maintain External Delivery ID and External Delivery Item ID manually. I believe only through custom solution can you maintain these automatically.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Deprecation of VBScript, its Impact on SAP GUI Scripting and Resulting Strategy
entry.content:
Hello Mattia @bergom,

JScript is the Microsoft implementation of the ECMA 262 language specification and it is a full implementation of the ECMAScript Edition 3 standard. It offers a few features which are not compliant with ECMA standards. One of this features is the method GetObject, to get a reference to an COM automation object, like the SAP GUI for Windows. No other approach that can do this will be available with Windows in the future after VBScript is discontinued. As far as I can tell, JScript was never used very much, so there is not that much information available. You can find the last documentation about WSH and JScript here. Rename the file from scrdoc56en.exe to scrdoc56en.cab and extract the file script56.chm with 7-zip. This help file contains a lot information about JScript. We see that the announcement to support JScript is obvious but does not seem to be strategically favorable from this perspective.

I could not find any information on exception 484. Therefore I suggest that you simply start with a JScript program and see if it works at all.

 

function main() {

  try {
    WScript.Echo("Hello World");
  } catch (exception) {
    WScript.Echo("An exception " + (exception.number & 0xFFFF) +
      " occurred:\n" + exception.description + "\n" + exception);
  }
}

main();

 

 If this works then simply copy in block by block and see where the error occurs.

Let us know your results.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content:
@JansiMurugesan Good job!Is there a more detailed description of the value stream and processes? and How to identify and map key processes within value streams? Many thanks!

Best regards,

Yuyao

entry.title: Re: Get the contracts list whose value is greater than 1...
entry.content:
Hi @ramsthota1986 

You need to create Measure/Account base dimension 

then use dimension as filter

Let me know if you need further information.

Regards

Bilal

entry.title: Re: How to Add a Nested JSON Object in RestService.SendRequest Action Body in SAP MDK?
entry.content: @Riyaz_abdulI am also facing the same issue, headers are getting removed.

entry.title: Re: Test system Refresh
entry.content:
Hi Chindhu,

The Empty Tenant refresh process is comprised of the following steps:

      1. An existing test tenant will be emptied of all residing data via this method.
      2. The URL and tenant ID of the tenant remains the same.

This means, all the data will be deleted from the tenant and the same tenant URL and tenant ID will remain as it is. You'll then have to rebuild your test tenant from scratch.

Please refer to the below KBA for more details:

2340655 - How to Request Tenant Refresh in SAP Business ByDesign and SAP Cloud for Customer 

 

Best Regards,

Darshan N

entry.title: PaymentPurpose does not appear for Payment Medium: JP_ZENGINKYO_I_XML
entry.content:
I selected SCB Indicator on the Journal Management  to process payment, but PaymentPurpose(/ Document/CstmrCdtTrfInitn/PmtInf/CdtTrfTxInf/RgltryRptg/Dtls/Inf/PaymentPurpose) is not output.

The trace shows no value for FPAYHX-REF02+64.

Do I need to make any settings?

entry.title: Re: RFC destination not showing up in destinations in Bui...
entry.content:
To have a destination show up in SAP Build Apps, you need the following properties:

BuildApps.Enabled = true

It used to be also you needed HTML5.DynamicDestination and WebIDEEnabled but perhaps those are not needed anymore for SAP Build Apps.

To have it defined as either plain REST or OData, you need one of the following:

BuildApps.ApiType = odata
BuildApps.ApiType = rest

See the documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Not Showing Description from OData v4 Metadata
entry.content:
HI @umayaraj 

there are some limitation in Odata 4. You can you try  /$metadata output to ensure labels are present.

secondly also try to add sap:semantics="text" alongside sap:label .let me know if you need further information

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning
entry.content:
Hi @vijaykumar108 

You can achieve this using Data Actions with an Advanced Formula that distributes the entered value based on the actuals from the previous year.

entry.title: Re: 2502 C4C Release Discussion
entry.content:
Can SAP provide more details on this one?

Ticket Management - Roundoffs

Visit is now created automatically when Appointment is created

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @dylan-drummond , first of all you are a gem for writing this extensive guide. Thank you so much for taking your time out to write this.

I just wanted to share some updates from my side. I have a Macbook Pro with an M4 Pro chip with 24GB RAM with Sequoia 15.3 installed. Following your guide I have managed to install the latest version 'abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01' via the UTM/QEMU path. The docker path is not working as of today, unfortunately.
Here are the settings I have used for anyone interested.
CPU Cores: 6
RAM: 20GB (Yes, RAM swapping does help)
Disk: 256GB

I really hope with the growing popularity of ARM64 processors, one day we will see native support for it.

entry.title: Re: Consume a custom CDS with parameters exposed as an OData API
entry.content:
Hi @StefanieSchulke 

I do not know that buffering would be the cause of why you noticed a difference in response times between your first and second call, although I must say I doubt that would be the reason.

Did you try it a 3rd, 4th, nth time?  did the response vary greatly still?

What if you added/amended data in your backend, did that also dramatically affect your response times?

It is possible that the first time you executed the query, some compilation occurred, and additional services were activated/deployed (not a factual answer, just a thought).

I would suggest maybe, that if in the future you see response times wildly changing, you use your browser developer tools to see where the issue could be, and if it is a hindrance on normal operations, raise a support issue (to provide factual content to your incident, I would also suggest that you provide trace information, and here maybe the support note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3405253 will help you to get it, if this is not familiar for you)

entry.title: Re: 2502 C4C Release Discussion
entry.content: I think for this the use case is if the service technician has a appointment with a customer than the system creates a visit for the technician wrt the appointment. It can be useful in planned maintenance and work tickets scenario. Anyhow it might be available as a scoping question which can be scoped if required.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA public Plant Abroad - 5RP scope item
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part1
entry.content:
I often get asked, "Can I use SAP Build Apps to upload files to the SAP Document Management Service and use them in SAP Build Process Automation processes?"
Although this is not impossible, there was a problem with uploading files from SAP Build Apps to DMS. (There is also a method using JavaScript, but it is not suitable as a NoCode tool.)

I thought of a way to upload without code, and wrote this article as a preparatory explanation, but in the meantime, a new feature was added to SAP Build Apps, and it has become much easier, so I would like to explain it.

Previous explanation: About the file attachment function of SAP Build Process Automation

When you use the file attachment feature in SAP Build Process Automation, the file is saved in SAP Document Management Service. Have you ever seen how files are stored on the SAP Document Management Service side? Actually, a folder is created for each file, and the files are stored in that folder. SAP Document Management Service assigns a unique ID to each folder and file for management purposes. In SAP Build Process Automation, attached files are handled using the (automatically created) folder ID rather than the file ID. This is correct

 

 

spa-res:cmis:folderid:<folder ID on DMS>

 

 

It is handled in this format.

In other words, if you upload a file to SAP Document Management Service with SAP Build Apps and want to use it in the workflow of SAP Build Process Automation, on the Build Apps side

  1. Create a folder on the SAP Document Management Service repository. At that time, obtain the ID issued to the folder.
  2. Upload the file to the above folder.
  3. When starting the SApBuild Process Automation workflow from SAP Build Apps, pass the file save destination information in the format "spa-res:cmis:folderid:<folder ID on DMS>"

That is necessary.

Preparation: Create a destination

Create a Destination to access SAP Document Management Service from SAP Build Apps.I will create this by cloning the Destination "sap_process_automation_document_store" in order to link SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Document Management Service. I thought it would be a good idea to set additional properties for use with SAP Build Apps in "sap_process_automation_document_store", but this is a Destination that has a specified method for collaboration and is automatically created. Therefore, although the content is almost the same, we will create another one for use from SAP Build Apps.

I cloned "sap_process_automation_document_store" and created a Destination called "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps".
This Destination has the following changes from "sap_process_automation_document_store".
URL: Add /root to the end
Furthermore, the following is added as Additional Properties.

BuildApps.ApiTyperest
BuildApps.Enabledtrue
HTML5.DynamicDestinationtrue
MobileEnabledtrue

Honestly, I don't think adding "/root" to the URL is necessary. However, when I checked the API used by Document Management Service in SAP Business Accelerator Hub, I decided that the URL with "/root" added could be used commonly, so I added it to reduce the effort. The items set in Additional Properties are the settings for using this Destination with SAP Build Apps.

 

I'll explain this while creating a simple app.

 

1. Creating a process on the SAP Build Process Automation side

First, create a workflow that will receive attachment information. Since this is for operation verification, I will create a very simple one.

The API trigger is used as trigger, and It will link from SAP Build Apps using the SAP Build Library .
Set "FolderId" as an input variable for the process.

Set a variable of type "DocumentFolder".

The form connected from the trigger is also simple.

Place only file objects. Please check the ReadOnly attribute for this file object.
Assign the "FolderId" set as an input variable to this form on the process.

Set the form Subject, Recipients User, etc. appropriately. For testing, let's set the Recipients User to be ourselves. Recipients User specifies "Process started by" in Process metadata.
Release and deploy. Remember to run Publish from the lobby so it is available from SAP Build Apps.

 

This is going to be long, so I'll continue with Part 2.

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part2
entry.content:
*This article is a continuation of Part 1.

2. Creating SAP Build Apps side

First, set two variables as Application Variables.

FolderId and FolderName. Create these two in text type. There is no problem with using Page Variable instead of Application Variable, so please use the appropriate one according to the actual purpose.

SAP Build Apps has prepared a simple screen like this.

 

There are only two buttons. You can probably guess what the button does from the contents of its label, but let's create the logic for each button.

Before that, let's install the new features of SAP Build Apps explained in Part 1.
Go to MARKET PLACE and search for "HTTP destination"

Install the module called "HTTP destination request". Follow the same steps to also search for "Pick Files" and install the "Pick Files" component as well.

3.Creating the logic for the Upload File button

Now let's create the logic for the Upload File button.
As explained in Part 1, in order to use files uploaded to DMS with SAP Build Process Automation, it is necessary to create a folder and upload the files there. So this button

  1. Select files to upload
  2. Create a folder in DMS (& get folder ID)
  3. Upload the file to the folder created in above

We will write the logic. The entire logic is below.

It would be too long to explain everything, so I will only explain the parts that I think are important.

1. Let the Pick Files control select files.
Here, please change "Pick multiple files?" in OPTIONAL INPUTS to False so that only one file can be selected.

2. Create(Generate) a folder name on DMS and set it to variable: FolderName.

For Assigned value, use Formula and set it as follows this time.

Make the folder name the timestamp value with the preposition "FromBuildApps-". I think this will probably be unique.

3. Create a folder on DMS using an HTTP destination request.
Create a folder on the DMS using an HTTP destination request.

For Destination, specify "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps" created in Part 1, for HTTP Method, specify POST, and for Request body type, specify multipart/form-data.
Specify the Request body using "List of values" as shown in the table below. If there is no cautionary note, set it as Static Text.

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateFolder
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]Select application variables : FolderName
propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:folder
succincttrue

４．Obtain the FolderId value from the return value of the folder creation API.
If the folder is created on DMS in Step 3 (if successful), the following value will be returned from the API.

 

 

{
  "succinctProperties":
    {
      "cmis:objectId": "fWtCFDH4ah2BGeFqzoorOLETNNq65cmp82KfYUSpWxA",
      "sap:parentIds": ["5daf1b700094ead661934376"],
      "cmis:name": "FromBuildApps-1737086879955",
      "cmis:lastModifiedBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:objectTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:lastModificationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:createdBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:baseTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:parentId": "5daf1b700094ead661934376",
      "sap:owner": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:creationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:changeToken": "0",
    },
  "exactACL": false,
}

 

 

The ObjectId of the folder that must be sent to the SAP Build Process Automation process is the value of "cmis:objectId". Assign this value to the variable FolderId.
This uses Formula,

 

STRING(<Output of HTTP destination request to create folder>.resBodyParsed.succinctProperties["cmis:objectId"])

 

You can obtain it by specifying . "<Output of HTTP destination request to create folder>.resBodyParsed" is correct if you select it from output of another node in Variables displayed on the left.

5. Upload files to DMS
Similar to Step 3, this uses the HTTP destination request to operate the DMS file upload API.
For Destination, specify "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps" created in Part 1, for HTTP Method, specify "POST", and for Request body type, specify "multipart/form-data".

Specify the Request body using "List of values" as shown in the table below. If there is no cautionary note, set it as Static Text.

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateDocument
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]

Use Formula:
Specify files[0].name of PickFiles - which created step1.

propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:document
filenamesame as PropertyValue[0]
_charset_UTF-8
includeAllowableActionstrue
succinctfalse
mediaUse Formula:
Specify files[0] of PickFiles - which created step1.

An important point to note is that in DMS, when uploading files to a folder, you must specify the path. This is a form in which the path name (folder name) is included in the API endpoint. Therefore, open the OPTIONAL INPUTS of the HTTP destination request and use the formula for Path to use the folder name added in the form "/"+appVars.FolderName.

OK, It will be able to upload to DMS.

The logic for the File Upload button is now complete. Next, we will develop the logic for the Execute Workflow button that will link to the SAP Build Process Automation process with the information of this uploaded file.

This is getting too long, so I'll continue with Part 3.

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part3
entry.content:
*This article is a continuation of Part 2. Here is part 1.

4.Creating the logic for the Execute Workflow button

Now that we have completed the logic to create a folder and upload files to DMS, we will create the logic to start the SAP Build Process Automation process from this SAP Build Apps application. It will start with the uploaded file (folder) information attached, so you will be able to reference this file on SAP Build Process Automation.

In SAP Build Apps, you can use the process created in Part 1 with SAP Build Library from Integrations.

If you can't see the deployed process in the list here, you've probably forgotten to Publish.

Create the logic for the Execute Workflow button. The logic is simple.

The important thing is the "Trigger Process". Select the process you made available from Integrations.

Select Object with Properties as Input Parameters and use Formula to set the values.

As explained in Part 1, the folder ID information must be passed in the format "spa-res:cmis:folderid:<folder ID on DMS>". The folder ID should have been assigned to Application Variables from the API return value when creating the folder. Concatenate the string "spa-res:cmis:folderid:" to that value.

The logic is now complete. Let's test it.

 

Test

Run the Build Apps application you created.

Clicking the File Upload button will open a file selection dialog, so this time we will upload a PDF document. I created a simple PDF document in Word for testing purposes.

The display depends on the settings in the dialog set after the file upload API, but it looks like the upload was successful. In debugging mode, it is better to check whether the folder is created and uploaded properly using ReuseUI or DMS API.
Next, click the Execute Workflow button to start the SAP Build Process Automation process.
This process (created in Part 1) is a structure that sends a form with a file attached to itself after being triggered by the API. Open My Inbox from SAP Build Lobby.

You should have received the form yourself. There seems to be an attached file as well. Let's check the contents.

You can confirm that it is a PDF file that you uploaded from SAP Build Apps.

In this way, you can upload files with SAP Build Apps and link them to the SAP Build Process Automation workflow. SAP Build Apps allows you to perform more application-like processing than SAP Build Process Automation. It is also possible to do things that are impossible with SAP Build Process Automation forms, such as narrowing down searches such as major classification -> medium classification -> minor classification -> product, and interactive collaboration with external systems. The recommended usage is to use SAP Build Apps on the application screen that triggers the workflow.

nother important point is that you can link files uploaded to DMS from other apps by passing the format "spa-res:cmis:folderid:" + folder ID when triggering a process. (Although it needs to be the same DMS repository used by SAP Build Process Automation)

From what we hear from customers about how they use it, it seems like a common pattern is to attach files to workflows. I think you can do a lot more with it, so please give it a try.

Regards,

entry.title: Logistic order for packing unit can't be created, when a canceled log. ord. exists for same PU.
entry.content:
Dear community,

when having a logistic order in status canceled or rejected for a specific packing unit, creating a new logistic order is not possible. Error message "Reference number already exists." pops up.

I am wondering if this is a bug? Because this would mean instead of canceling a faulty log ord, I have to book it all the way to completed, just to be able to create a new log ord.

Thank you in advance,
Stephan

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Not Showing Description from OData v4 Metadata
entry.content:
hi @chmbilal , thanks for your support .

 

sample metadata below , and in sac description is not show as expected , any other idea ?

<edmx:Edmx Version="4.0">
<edmx:DataServices>
<Schema Namespace="ME2M_ME23N_SVR">
<EntityType Name="MANAGE">
<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="EBELN"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="EBELN" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="150" sap:columnSize="150" sap:label="Purchasing Document" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="true" sap:required="false" sap:Position="1" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="AEDAT" Type="Edm.Date" Nullable="true" sap:columnSize="110" sap:label="Created On" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="true" sap:required="false" sap:Position="2" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="BSART" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="130" sap:columnSize="130" sap:label="Purchasing Doc.Type" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="false" sap:required="false" sap:Position="3" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="BSTYP" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="130" sap:columnSize="130" sap:label="Purch.Doc.Category" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="false" sap:required="false" sap:Position="4" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="BUKRS" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="120" sap:columnSize="120" sap:label="Company Code" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="true" sap:required="false" sap:Position="5" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="EKGRP" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="120" sap:columnSize="120" sap:label="Purchasing Group" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="true" sap:required="false" sap:Position="6" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="EKORG" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="150" sap:columnSize="150" sap:label="Purch.Organization" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="true" sap:required="false" sap:Position="7" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="ERNAM" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="120" sap:columnSize="120" sap:label="Created By" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="false" sap:required="false" sap:Position="8" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="FRGZU" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="140" sap:columnSize="140" sap:label="Release Status" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="false" sap:required="false" sap:Position="9" sap:isNeed="true"/>
<Property Name="INCO1" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="90" sap:columnSize="90" sap:label="Incoterms" sap:semantics="text" sap:quickinfo="" sap:visible="true" sap:filterable="false" sap:required="false" sap:Position="10" sap:isNeed="true"/>

entry.title: Error code 067(PG), error message "both amount and quantity entries not permitted"
entry.content:
Hi,

I have done an upload for recurring deductions retrospectively from 2023 on this Jan payroll and am getting error "both amount and quantity entries not permitted" All the parameters are correct and the file neither had   amount nor quantity. How do I correct the error for data to replicate? 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: FM-Advance payment needs to reduce the consumables and not to reduced from the migo in Budcon FM.
entry.content:
Dear All FM Expert,

I am looking for your expert guidance for my Indian Govt client .

The advance payment is made against the "multi year" purchase order for the project with WBS (RA Bill).

For Eg.  Currently, consumables Budget is INR 1000, advance paid INR. 300(with PO and WBS),Purchase order is 700.

Advance payment has not shown any changes in the Budcon report.

Consumables Consumed AvailableCurrent BudgetCommitment
     1000100010001000700

 After raising the service entry/ migo of 500 .The amount is reduced from the Migo amount .The Budcon is showing the below scenario.

1000200(500-300)8001000700

My client wants to show this payment reduced the consumables and not from Migo amount .

10003007001000700

So that, they will have a correct available balance for the consumption. To get this scenario, they are not entering the WBS or po in the advance payment. But this will loose the tracking the payment against the project.

These are all long term projects which can be completed in the future date and the payments are adjusted by mutual consideration with vendors, based on their request/ financial conditions. These payments are not adjusted against Miro or Migo amount.

Can you guide me to achieve the above output as per client's requirement with WBS/PO entry in the advance payment?

Thank You in advance!

Warm Regards,

Raja P.

entry.title: Re: How to delete unwanted flavours from /PERSONAS/ADMIN...
entry.content:
HI.

Quite easy:

You need to be the owner

 

And no role assignments

entry.title: Re: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Innovation Guide
entry.content: @Hariharan-Gandhi May be an unrelated question - I am looking to log the user id ( from req.user.id ) associated to all requests logged - Any clue how I can achieve that ?

entry.title: Re: Basics of sustainability solution SAP Product and REACH Compliance
entry.content:
Great insights and a well-structured explanation of SAP Product and REACH Compliance!
The detailed breakdown of compliance processes, BOM transfer, and regulatory management makes it easier to understand the solution's scope.
Appreciate the effort in putting this together!

I also noticed that you have created process documentation with 200+ pages, which sounds incredibly valuable.
I am very interested in accessing this documentation—could you please guide me on how I can get it?

Looking forward to your response! 😊

Regards

Ajit

entry.title: Re: No found message error when deploy to sap_java_buildpack
entry.content:
Hello @YOJAN 

 

Sometimes choosing the buildpack as per

buildpacks:
  - sap_java_buildpack
is not enough.
You need to choose also the environment which will be used to run your application like Tomcat or TomEE.
You can check wiki about sap_java_buildpacks here: buildpacks (I assume in your case TomEE should be used)
 
You can try modify your manifest.yml
 
---
applications:
- name: demoapp
random-route: true
path: target\demoapp-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.war
memory: 1024M
buildpacks:
     - sap_java_buildpack
env:
     TARGET_RUNTIME: tomee7
     JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: '{ "jres": ["com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jdk.SAPJVMJDK"] }'

After changing the manifest.yml deploy again your application.
cf login (if logged out)
cf push (from the directory where the manifest file is located)
cf logs demoapp --recent (for checking the logs)
 
After that you can try to access the servlet, but not on :http://localhost:8080/test.
This is localhost, but your app is deployed "somewhere" on CLoud Foundry so you need to check your api endpoint in your BTP CF account and to use it for accessing your application.
 
Best regards,
Kristiyan

entry.title: Re: How to add Snowflake as a source for SAP Datasphere or SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Sefan_Linders  and @javierc ,

This issue is fixed with two connection parameters and latest version of both dpagent and snowflake driver.

Br//Rajesh G

entry.title: Re: Control for the supplier invoice

entry.title: Re: Upload files to specific Folders inside SAP DMS from SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: The user wants a warning popup message to appear after inputting a custom field at the header level
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

Some custom fields have been created at the header level of the Sales Billing screen (VF01), including a field for the Bill of Lading Date. The user wants a warning popup message to appear after inputting this field, saying: "Please reverify the Date." I have tried multiple BADIs, but it does not seem to be possible. Could you please confirm if this is achievable or suggest any possible solutions?

entry.title: Re: Oracle create inventory issue
entry.content: One known issue mentioned in SAP KBA ##2879640. If not helps, can you please share the installation logs?

entry.title: Re: Benefits of Executing Stored Procedures via API in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content: Nice explanation. Waiting for the APIs to trigger the stored procedure.

entry.title: Re: FSM - The Query API returns fewer rows than specified...
entry.content:
Dear Reporter,

Please refer https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_query_api/offset-or-limit.html

Attention: Due to memory issues and latencies, OFFSET / LIMIT is NOT supported in the Query API. It is recommended to use the Analytics & Reporting app to determine the number of records returned in a query.

If you use same query in Analytics & Reporting app - it will give you desired behavior

In Admin - Query API 

If Records >100 --> 50 records will be shown on UI [But if you click on Export - you will see more than 100 records]

 

Hope this helps! 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to assign source of supply for all line items in...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

To make system propose source of supply automatically for sales order items, you need to do below:

1)In Business configuration, in the third step "Scoping" of project scope, expand "Purchasing", ensure that "Third-Party Procurement of Materials" is selected within "Purchase Request and Order Management".

2)In Business Configuration, in the fourth step "Questions", expand "Selling Products and Services", select "Sales Orders", mark question "Do you want to immediately confirm quantities and requested dates during sales order creation based on an availability check?" in scope.

3)For materials which you hope source of supply can be proposed automatically, go to the master data of these materials, under tab Availability Confirmation, select corresponding planning area, ensure "Disable Automatic Ship-From Determination" is not ticked.

Please refer to help document "Assigning a Source of Supply": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cf1f0e0722d1014956cff630ded4823.html

Kind Regards,
Henry
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to define custom geographic areas in a SAP Analyt...
entry.content:
Hi Pedro,
I do not have idea of adding custom shapes. can you try to check buble if that working to confrim if its shape issue or some thing else.

entry.title: Re: Upload files to specific Folders inside SAP DMS from...
entry.content:
You can use an automation -- download the file to an agent and then move it to a different folder. Admittedly, a long way to go for something that should be configurable. As far as I know it is not configurable.

At the very least, you should be able to use an action to move the document, but actions do not support multipart calls, but maybe they will enable that (as they recently did from within SAP Build Apps).

entry.title: Re: About BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Have a seen this blog? https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/999-the-number-of-the-bseg/ba-p/13692857? Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors OData API purgeType=full not working....
entry.content: The solution could be in the EmpEmploymentTermination entity that does have the upsertable enddate field.

entry.title: Get All cost centers based on date&time creation through standard API
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I'm trying to consume SAP "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" Web API that related to "Get cost centers" or "Get profit centers" by standard official SAP API links:

https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_PROFITCENTER_0001-v1/overview

https://api.sap.com/api/CE_COSTCENTER_0001/overview

Both web API services need to submit primary keys such as controlling area, Cost center id, and validity end date(please refer to attached screenshot)

Is it possible to retrieve all cost centers through the API that were created in my tenant (for integration and data mapping with an external system) without specifying the required request keys?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Can we unsend/ delete the Bills transferred to S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello Dhanashree,

yes, it is possible to re-transfer a bill. 
It requires some manual steps:
1) in S/4H: reject or delete the BDR(s) which has been created for the bill
2) in Subscription Billing: reset the transfer status of the bill within the 'Manage Billing Data' app. This will delete the links to BDR(s) from the bill and sets back the transfer status to 'Not Transferred'

Afterwards the bill will be re-transferred  
 
Kind regards
Ralf

entry.title: Re: ME21N Document overview within the Selection variant
entry.content:
hello,

first go somewhere here:

then put some values and save as your variant

execute 

it will appear here

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @AnkanSikdar ,

good that you got it working with lower RAM allocation than SAP recommends, as it brings more MacBooks into the "Feasible Devices" set.

My guess is that since around macOS 14.4 (spring 2024) when Apple tightened Rosetta security to prevent e.g. emulated Java processes issuing taskkill type commands, this unfortunately for "Docker path for Abap Trial with Rosetta" means it is unlikely to ever work again. So there is only the UTM/QEMU path available, but at least that one works even though it is of course a bit slow-running (as full emulation of the VM).

It could happen, that in the future Apple actually drops Rosetta, as the idea behind Rosetta is that it is a "transition period"-technology until some critical mass of macOS app providers have switched to providing arm64-versions of their stuff... and in general this critical mass already exists... alternatively, Apple might choose to keep providing Rosetta... I have no insight into what they will actually do.

For Abap Trial, maybe if the popularity of Windows on arm64 increases (as many predict), then SAP will have more motivation to provide an arm64 Docker image of Abap Trial. @julieplummer20 might have some insight or view as to whether this idea which I raised last year, has some chance of getting onto the Abap Trial product roadmap?

entry.title: Re: what is deffernce between warehouse request, warehous...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The three documents are always in the same document flow in inbound/outbound/internal logistics scenario. Their relationship and difference are:

1) The sequence of the three documents in a document flow is warehouse request > warehouse order > warehouse confirmation.
2) Literally, warehouse request is a request to execute warehouse tasks, warehouse order is created from warehouse request for warehouse management to manage warehouse tasks, while warehouse confirmation records what has been done in warehouse each time.

For more details please refer to help document "Outbound Delivery Processing": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cec2009722d1014b1b49cdebbbb9bc3.html

Kind Regards,
Henry
SAP Support

entry.title: material availability date is before sales order creation date
entry.content:
Hello,

We are experiencing issues with a few sales orders where the material availability date is being determined before the order creation date.

We have checked the scheduling settings for the sales document type in SPRO, and backward scheduling is not enabled.

What could be the possible reason for this?

Thank you in advance for your response.

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on pa...
entry.content:
Hello,

I found solution based on Prefilling Fields Using the DefaultValuesFunction - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK
I created in backend function with parameters which contains keys of parent entity. That function is called before create new instance of entity and fields which are used for field for value help were prefilled. You even shouldn't show these fields on the screen. I don't know how to was implemented frontend part because unfortunately I'm not full stack developer. But I think experienced fullstack or frontend developer can understand what he/she should do based on help which I added above.

In behavior of entity which is going to use predefined fields add row
static function SetDefaultFieldsForFilter parameter zi_abstract_entity_with_parent_keys result [1] $self;

Then in behavior pool class implement corresponding method as such

LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<key>).

READ ENTITIES OF zi_root_entity IN LOCAL MODE

ENTITY root_entity

ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( rootentitykey= <key>-%param-rootentitykey) )

RESULT DATA(lt_root_instances).

 

ASSIGN lt_root_instances[ 1 ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ri>).

 

IF sy-subrc <> 0.

CONTINUE.

ENDIF.

 

APPEND VALUE #( %cid = <key>-%cid

%param-keyofchildentity = <key>-%param-keyofchildentity

%param-fieldfromroot1forfilter1= <ri>-fieldfromroot1

%param-fieldfromroot2forfilter2= <ri>-fieldfromroot2

%param-currencycode = c_currency_eur "if you want to add definite value for field) TO result.

 

ENDLOOP.

It is look more like workaround, but I didn't find other way how to use fields from parent entity for value help in child entity as filter during creation of new instance of child entity in managed without draft application.

P.S. Sorry that I didn't wrap code into code tag but I didn't find how to do that. Maybe somebody hint me ho to do that 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Not Showing Description from OData v4 Metadata
entry.content: are you using live model? if yes than check if the field coming in import data option. if the issue still presist, suggestion is to create separate filed for description

entry.title: Calculation View Input Parameter Order Not Respected in Analysis for Office
entry.content:
In my Calculation View, I have three input parameters: Start date, End date, and plant. However, when I execute it in Analysis for Office, the parameters appear in a different order—first end date, then plant, and finally start date. I have already tried adjusting the order in the Calculation View, and while it works correctly there, the sequence remains unchanged in Analysis for Office.

I would like to know if there is a way to control the order of parameters when running a Calculation View directly in Analysis for Office.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Chart Display Issues with SAP BW Live Queries in SAC
entry.content: get the DataSource measure and create a new 2d array with measuser id and description. and manipulate it as you want to do.

entry.title: Forcing a Refresh of M_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_STATISTICS Statistics
entry.content:
Good Afternoon,

I have purged a table named ZEXAMPLE stored in SAPHANADB.

I check the disk size of this table with the following query:
SQL> select TABLE_NAME, DISK_SIZE from M_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_STATISTICS where SCHEMA_NAME='SAPHANADB' and TABLE_NAME='ZEXAMPLE';

But this query returns an old value, that does not take into account my deletions.

1. Would you know which Hana Job refreshes the table sizes statistics ?

2. How could I force a refresh of the M_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_STATISTICS for the table SAPHANADB.ZEXAMPLE ?

3. How could I force a refresh of the statistics for the tables that belong to the Schema SAPHANADB ?

Have a nice day.

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
entry.content: @danielmacho72 indeed, it was mentioned in the video but it is not appearing in the official roadmap yet.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Build: Create Business Apps, Processes and website without Code(Chennai, India)
entry.content: when can we expect the confirmation mail for 7th jan event?

entry.title: SAC - BW4 Live - problems with "visibility of structure members" variable after BW upgrade
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

just wanted to ask if somebody has experienced similar problems.

After Upgrading from BW4 2.0 to BW4 2023 SPS03 we have a problem that BW4 "visibility of structure members" with variable, does not work properly anymore in SAC stories with BW4 live connection.


The correct colomns are shown initially when we open the story, but when we change the prompt (for example with Table.getDataSource().openPromptDialog() or the built-in button), it is not reflected in the Story. SAC does not update which columns are shown. It does still work in AfO after the upgrade, so I guess it is some kind of "ina" issue. It used to work before the upgrade. And it still works on our Production System which is not upgraded yet.

Implementation of note 3526839 improved the problem a bit. Now the correct colomns are shown on startup, but not when data is refreshed in SAC. Before the implementation of this note all colomns where always shown.

We also implemented "3523226 - INA : Incorrect variable values in the update section of the auto variable submit call." and many more notes after the upgrade;

I opened a ticket/case with SAP, but perhaps the community is quicker. If you experienced similar issues please let us know.

Thanks and best regards

Dominik

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
You need to:

  • define an environment variable of type destination
  • Add this activity before you call any other DMS activities:
  • Then when you run (or deploy) you need to specify a destination. I use a destination that ends with the repo ID I want to use, and then I add /root or whatever else I need in the DMS activities when I need to specify a path. 

As for the path, yes, I think there is a missing activity allowing you to get folder content with object ID. But you can do it the "old" way directly with Web Service Call with Destination, which you can see examples of if you go to the store and import the template "How to use Document Management Service".

You'd call the Get Properties API - https://api.sap.com/api/GetPropertiesApi/path/get_browser__repository_id__root and you can filter for just the name.

Here's how it looks in Postman:

And here's how it looks in a project that gets the folder name:

Perhaps there's an easier way. I'm pretty sure development was working on adding that activity.

entry.title: Re: List report is not getting refreshed after delete operation?
entry.content: List Report Fiori application automatically refreshes after delete. Please check console if there is any error while rebinding

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @matthias_schmalz,
thank you for your answer.
I hope this diagram explain properly what I'm trying to do.

As you mentioned, each app is a standalone application with its own AppRouter, SAPUI5 application, and services. I have tried using shared services to communicate with the library, but without success.

I assume I need to modify your example to make it work properly in standalone mode. When you say I need an HTML page, do you mean a standard web page or just the sap-ui-bootstrap script specifying the library resources?
Would the library require its own approuter?

Thanks in advance.

Regards.

Carlos

entry.title: Re: Minimum Viable Product with Seamless Planning
entry.content:
Hi @fkatzdobler, 

Thanks a lot for this great blogpost! I am always very glad to read from proven experts that they support our strategic direction!

In case somebody is wondering, Franz carried out a manual migration of content to a newly provisioned SAP Analytics Cloud tenant on HANA Cloud. Timelines for the central migration carried out by SAP will be shared in this blogpost.

entry.title: Re: Table Z does not exist in the database
entry.content:
I've got the same error when I changed some the data elements...

I noticed that when activating the DB, SAP creates a temporary table. In my case, I modified the DB several times and SAP created several indexes in these temporary tables (there was no index in the transparent table) and that's why it didn't activate.

I solved on SE14 -> "Extra -> Invalid Temporary Table". Delete all temporary table, go back to SE11 and activate your table

Alander Fosse.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Update Child entity from Parent entity with...
entry.content:
Try to set the control field as well like below and it should work:MODIFY ENTITIES OF c_parent_entity IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY child_entity
UPDATE FIELDS ( status )
WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys
( %tky = key-%tky
status = task_status-closed
%control = VALUE #( status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on) ) )
MAPPED mapped
FAILED failed
REPORTED reported.

entry.title: Re: Export options for S_ALR* report with SAP GUI for HTM...
entry.content:
In case someone else runs into this problem. In our environment, we managed to solve it with this workaround:

In the user parameters we maintained:

GXDexport.xls
G_RW_DOCUMENT_TYPE 
GRWEX XXXXX XEX

This caused:

- The export mode to be set, so that the 'export report' button is visible by default :

The output file would be automatically named 'export.xls', which in return gave a decent Excel file:

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content: Hi Dan, Thanks for your inputs. My doubt is related to destination, we have only one destination for DMS - "Sap_process_automation_document_store". We need to give this destination name?

entry.title: Splitting Warehouse tasks
entry.content: There are one or more line items in a delivery with 48 EA quantity total. and there are carts of capacity 8 EA. while picking has to create (split the delivery) warehouse tasks based on carts capacity and assign to each cart.

entry.title: Re: 2 Years Quarterly Historical Data Upload Approach
entry.content: Agree with Florian. I will just add here that if Q1 is correct Q2 has the correct periodic value you should be able to report correct using YTD measure. Br Martin Jul

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Great blog Martin, and nice work on the detailing.

 

I am working on a similair setup in which I am provisioning users from Entra ID to onprem S/4HANA.

As the CIS subject is still rather new to me I am trying to find out what is the best setup to use. You are pushing users from EntraID to CIS. As we do not have so much control over the customer Entra ID I have setup the EntraID as source and IAS as target so I can do my transformation in CIS. This gave me a challenge with the SAP ABAP username as it by default gets created with the EntraID login, which has an email and is too long. I managed to transform it to the P number of IAS, not very nice, but it works.

My latest challenge is that the user is created in S/4, the business partner is created, saml2 is working with CIS, but I am now faced with an initial password screen in my fiori login.

Do you know how to disable the password of a user when creating ABAP users from CIS?

 

thank you

Sander

entry.title: SAP Signavio Community Expert Session - Aligning Strategy with Performance through Key and Process I
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business environment, aligning strategic goals with operational performance is essential for successful business transformations and Business Process Management (BPM) initiatives. This session, led by Alex and Taryn, will dive into the importance of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and Process Performance Indicators (PPIs) as tools for steering organizational change. You’ll learn how KPIs set benchmarks tied to strategic objectives, while PPIs provide detailed insights into process efficiency. By integrating these metrics within a cohesive framework, organizations can align their transformation efforts with strategic priorities and adapt quickly to operational challenges.

This session will provide practical guidance on selecting, designing, and implementing KPIs and PPIs to drive performance, foster continuous improvement, and ensure long-term success in transformation initiatives.

Why Attend?

- Strategic Alignment: Learn how Key and Process Indicators bridge the gap between strategy and operational performance.
- Practical Insights: Gain hands-on knowledge for selecting and designing effective indicators.
- Continuous Improvement: Discover how to use metrics for ongoing process optimization.
- Interactive Q&A: Engage with experts Alex and Taryn and share insights with peers.

Register today to secure your spot!


Please note that our Expert Sessions are live-only events and will not be recorded, so we encourage you to join us in real-time.

entry.title: Re: Auto Technical Complete for Production Order
entry.content: Hi @Dave_He , thank you for the response! So we have to create a custom program that run the mass TECO for production? with some logic that see production order that pass due date? Thank You!

Hi Ravi, i have exactly the same requirement to have optional parameters for storage type when reading data from table LINK.  

where link.lgtyp = $parameters.p_LGTYP or :p_LGTYP is null

I dont have any syntax errors but it gives the below error when activating the view. This where condition is causing the problem. Please help.

Object of type CL_QLAST_EXPRESSION is undefined in calling method SET_ONE_CONDITION

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning Access: "error: unable to ret...
entry.content: We have also recently started seeing this error. The first case was reported on January 21. I have opened a ticket with SAP. One of our employees said she sees the error while using the Edge browser, but not Chrome.

entry.title: Re: Problem Doing ODATA Request with XCSRF Token
entry.content: Best is probably to activate the ICM trace on backend side, here you can sometimes see details of why it is responding with 401. And the Traffic Trace in the SCC can also help, then you see the complete HTTP request and can compare what is different between Postman and axios.

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content: No, you do not have to name it anything special -- though I would try to use the same destination you used for setting up DMS and Process Automation. You select the destination when you deploy or test the automation, so it can be called anything.

entry.title: How can I add a business area to a specific profit center?
entry.content: Hello, in SAP FI module I need to add a business area to a profit center. How can I do this? Which transaction?

entry.title: SAP CPI - How to Configure Deployed Secure Parameter in HTTP Adapter Authentication
entry.content:
In this Article, I will be explaining how we can use the deployed secure parameter in the http outbound adapter in SAP CPI.

We will be passing the authorization token stored in Secure parameter as Header in HTTP Outbound for authentication.

Solution - 

Step 1 - 

1. Navigate to Monitor.

2. Click the Security Material under Manage Security section

3. Choose Create & Select Secure Parameter. 




 

 

Enter the Name, Description & the secure parameter (In this case it would you Authorization Token)

4. Click on Deploy

5. When you refresh the Manage Security Material, the new entry is displayed in the table.

 

Step 2 - 

Before the HTTP Outbound adaptor, add a groovy script in the interface.
Use the below mentioned groovy code which will retrieve the authorization token from the deployed secure parameter and store it in Header during runtime.

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.*
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.*
import groovy.json.*


def Message processData(Message message) {

    def secureStorageService =  ITApiFactory.getService(SecureStoreService.class, null)

    //To get the deployed secure paramenter stored with the alias name
    def secureParameter = secureStorageService.getUserCredential("Alias Name") 

    //store the secure paramnter in apikey variable
    def apikey = secureParameter.getPassword().toString()

    //store it in Header with name "ApplicationAuthorization"
    message.setHeader("ApplicationAuthorization", apikey) 
    
    return message;
}

 

 

Mention in Request header under Connection for HTTP Outbound adapter

 

Conclusion - 

1. During the Interface runtime, the deployed secure parameter will be called based on the alias name configured in the groovy script.

2. Keep the confidential data like Password, Authorization token, API Key in a secure store & Safe.

entry.title: One month Pension Benefit, available always for enrolment for all including enrolled employees
entry.content:
Requirement:

Unique Pension Benefit that will always be available for enrolment including benefit enrolled employees for next month enrolment, employee to add the contribution only once for each month and one time deduction happens in compensation.

Possible Solution:

  • Create new benefit as Pension benefit type with all mandatory fields

  • Create Pension fund with only employee pay component, as shown in below picture and assign it to new 1 month pension benefit.

  • Create employee contribution business rule which will default minimum employee contribution amount by using rule scenario “Determine Contribution Amount for Pension/Retirement Savings Plan” and assign it to the employee pension contribution rule

  • Create new benefit schedule, monthly schedule periods with enrollment open, close dates and enrolment valid dates as shown in below picture and assign the “1Month schedule” benefit schedule to benefit.

This setup completes required configuration and its now time for testing.

Testing:

Benefit behaves as per the requirement:  

  • Benefit enrolment on 24th Jan will auto display effective from following month i.e. feb.2025:

     

  • Benefit enrolment automatically inactivates on following month of enrolment (i.e. inactivates enrolment from March.2025):

  • 1 time pension contribution component is removed automatically from March.2025: 

  • Always available for next month enrolment, 1time pension is not listed as active enrolment as of 5th feb.2025:

entry.title: Re: Buil Workzone Trail POC
entry.content: Admin Console is a feature of the advanced edition, which is not available on trial.

entry.title: Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue)
entry.content:
Scrapping:

Scrapping in SAP EWM refers to the process of removing damaged or obsolete inventory from the warehouse. This action updates stock levels and ensures that unusable goods are not part of future transactions. SAP EWM allows scrapping to be done efficiently by associating it with movement types and reasons.

 

Post Unplanned Goods Issue:

Posting an unplanned goods issue in SAP EWM occurs when goods are issued from inventory without a prior order or plan. This process helps adjust stock levels and manage unforeseen circumstances like damage or errors. SAP allows tracking of unplanned goods issues to ensure accurate inventory management and reporting.

 

Warehouse Number:

The warehouse number uniquely identifies a specific warehouse within the system. It is used to manage inventory, define storage locations, and configure operational processes. SAP EWM leverages the warehouse number to support various warehouse-specific functions and ensure efficient warehouse management.

 

Storage Type:

Storage type is the classification of physical storage areas within a warehouse, such as shelves, bins, or pallets. It helps organize goods based on their characteristics and requirements, like temperature or size. SAP EWM uses storage types to define the layout of the warehouse and optimize space utilization.

 

Storage Bin:

A storage bin is a specific location within a warehouse used to store goods. It is part of the warehouse structure and is defined by its unique identifier, ensuring accurate tracking of materials. SAP EWM assigns goods to storage bins for efficient stock management and retrieval.

Movement Reason:


Movement reasons are used to specify the cause or purpose of a goods movement, such as transferring, scrapping, or replenishing inventory. It helps track the context behind inventory transactions, ensuring transparency in warehouse operations. SAP EWM provides predefined movement reasons for various processes to support reporting and analysis.

 

Steps to perform Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue)

 

Step 1:

Enter the transaction code /N/SCWM/ADGI

 

 

 

Step 2:

In the post unplanned goods issue screen provide the below details:

  1. Warehouse number
  2. Storage Type
  3. Storage Bin
  4. GI Process
  5. Movement Reason

And Uncheck “Select scarp stock only”

 

Then click on the execute button.

 

 

Step 3:

In the next step we need to post items

Select the line item you wish to scrap and click on the Post GI button.

 

Scrapping is completed.

 

Conclusion:

Scrapping process using ADGI (post unplanned goods issue) is performed.
